reasoning: The CBOE Crude Oil Volatility Index (OVX) recently spiked to 39.37 as of August 1, 2025, which is notably higher than its baseline range of 30–35 over the past year. Since volatility indexes are strongly mean-reverting, a high recent value generally predicts an imminent decrease. The FRED statistical tool indicated an ~0.29 probability of the index exceeding 39.37 slightly over a week from now. Although there are occasionally higher spikes (e.g., to ~48 or ~56 in varying months over the past year), they are relatively short-lived. Following the instructions to increase uncertainty over time and recognizing the long-term base rate of OVX exceeding roughly 39 is around 35-42%, I set the probability for the immediate date around the FRED estimate (0.29) and progressively push it closer to the baseline probability of random spikes and toward 0.5 for the later dates.
